---
title: "Step 1. Ecosystem type and Extent"
numbered: false
format: html
bibliography: references.bib
---

The functional group (IUCN GET level 3 classifications and tool) for the Project Extent (part of the Yucatan Peninsula, Mexico) was inaccurate and data was deficient. Thus, the data used to define the ecosystem types of the Project Extent ([Table 5](#table5)) was based on research and literature [@jung2020]. The data was based on the IUCN Global Database that was later created and used for the methodology.

<details>

<summary>Table 5. Project Extent</summary>

::: {#table5}
:::

<div class="terra-table-title">
Table 5. Project Extent
</div>

```{r echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(DT)

table_data <- data.frame(
    check.names = FALSE,
  
  Realm = c(
    "Terrestrial"
  ),
  
  Biome = c(
    "Tropical and Sub-Tropical Forests"
  ),
  
  Ecosystem = c(
    "Tropical/Sub-tropical dry forests"
  ),
  
  `Project Extent (ha)` = c(
    "122,158"
  )
)

datatable(
  table_data,
  options = list(
    paging = FALSE,
    searching = FALSE,
    info = FALSE,
    ordering = FALSE,
    scrollX = TRUE,
    autoWidth = FALSE,
    columnDefs = list(
      list(className = 'dt-center', targets = "_all")
    )
  ),
  rownames = FALSE,
  class = "stripe hover compact"
)
```

</details>
